Metadata.title: STEM-ESD Computer Simulation
Metadata.description: Computer simulation website for STEM-ESD learning

Home.title: Home
Home.heading: STEM-ESD Computer Simulation
Home.description: The STEM-ESD computer simulation is a web-based science learning media designed to address environmental issues and climate change contextually. Through interactive activities integrating STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) aspects and based on Education for Sustainable Development (ESD), this media is expected to help students understand science while improving climate change literacy needed in the current era.
Home.viewCpTp: View CP & TP before starting the simulation
Home.feature1Desc: Real environmental scenarios visualized through simulations so students actively explore concepts.
Home.feature2Desc: Connecting science, technology, engineering, and mathematics to build comprehensive understanding.
Home.feature3Desc: Fostering awareness and real action on climate change and sustainability issues.

CpTp.title: CP & TP
CpTp.subtitle: Chapter: Environmental Issues
CpTp.heading: STEM-ESD Computer Simulation Media
CpTp.description: Designed based on Learning Outcomes (CP) and Learning Objectives (TP) so students can understand the connection between Earth systems, human activities, and sustainable solutions through interactive simulations.
CpTp.cpDesc: Students are able to analyze the relationship between Earth systems and human activities and their impacts on the environment, as well as design solutions to reduce these negative impacts as part of sustainable development efforts.
CpTp.tpDesc: Focus on concept understanding, impact analysis, policy evaluation, and STEM-based mitigation strategy design.
CpTp.detail1Title: Greenhouse effect mechanism and its relation to global warming
CpTp.detail1Desc: Students can explain the greenhouse effect mechanism using visual representations in computer simulations.
CpTp.detail2Title: Analysis of human activity effects on Earth's temperature rise
CpTp.detail2Desc: Students analyze the impact of fossil fuel use, deforestation, and transportation on temperature changes.
CpTp.detail3Desc: Students evaluate renewable energy, reforestation, and emission reduction based on simulation results.
CpTp.detail4Title: STEM-based climate change mitigation strategy design
CpTp.detail4Desc: Students design mitigation strategies using simulation results as a basis for sustainable decisions.

AktivitasSiswa.title: Student Activities
AktivitasSiswa.subtitle: Project Based Learning
AktivitasSiswa.heading: Student Activities: STEM-ESD Project
AktivitasSiswa.description: In this activity, students are invited to understand environmental issues and design sustainable solutions in project form. The science concepts gained through computer simulations about global warming and climate change are used as a basis for designing sustainable solutions using the STEM-ESD approach. The project is carried out through Engineering Design Process (EDP) stages so students can apply science concepts practically and systematically.
AktivitasSiswa.goal1: Identify environmental problems of climate change due to human activities.
AktivitasSiswa.goal2: Explain the cause-effect relationship of global warming and climate change scientifically.
AktivitasSiswa.goal3: Compare various alternative solutions for climate change mitigation.
AktivitasSiswa.goal4: Design solutions based on Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM).
AktivitasSiswa.goal5: Demonstrate environmental responsibility in accordance with Education for Sustainable Development (ESD) principles.
AktivitasSiswa.presentationDesc: After the prototype is completed and tested, each group prepares presentation materials to present the process, results, and impact of solutions developed using the STEM-ESD approach.
AktivitasSiswa.edpTitle: Project Flow Following Engineering Design Process (EDP)
AktivitasSiswa.edpAskDesc: Identify problems (energy waste, waste, carbon emissions), solution goals, and material, cost, time, and environmental impact constraints.
AktivitasSiswa.edpResearchDesc: Gather information from various sources about causes, impacts, and solutions to climate change.
AktivitasSiswa.edpImagineDesc: Generate creative ideas to design STEM-ESD based climate change mitigation products/systems.
AktivitasSiswa.edpPlanDesc: Choose the most effective idea, create sketches, work steps, materials, and how the solution works.
AktivitasSiswa.edpCreateDesc: Create a prototype according to the plan by applying science, technology, engineering, and mathematics concepts.
AktivitasSiswa.edpTestDesc: Test prototype performance, collect data, and assess effectiveness and sustainability.
AktivitasSiswa.edpImproveDesc: Evaluate strengths/weaknesses and improve the design for better optimization and impact.
AktivitasSiswa.assessmentDesc: Projects are assessed based on the following aspects:
AktivitasSiswa.assess1Desc: The product shows new ideas to help reduce the impact of climate change. Students can explain why the product is important for sustainability.
AktivitasSiswa.assess2Desc: The product can be used easily and safely. Tools/solutions work as intended and provide real benefits.
AktivitasSiswa.assess3Desc: The product is designed creatively in terms of form, function, or operation. Students combine science ideas with STEM aspects to create effective and efficient solutions.
AktivitasSiswa.assess4Desc: The product is strong and neat enough. It can be used more than once and still functions well.

EfekRumahKaca.title: Greenhouse Effect
EfekRumahKaca.subtitle: Environmental Issue
EfekRumahKaca.heading: Greenhouse Effect
EfekRumahKaca.shortDesc: Global warming due to increased greenhouse gases in the atmosphere.
EfekRumahKaca.longDesc: The greenhouse effect is defined as the process of rising Earth's temperature caused by changes in atmospheric composition, causing sunlight to remain on Earth and not be perfectly reflected back out of the atmosphere. The greenhouse effect occurs due to human activities that produce exhaust gases such as carbon dioxide (CO₂), dinitrogen oxide (N₂O), water vapor (H₂O), and methane (CH₄). These gases accumulate in the atmosphere, causing solar energy to be trapped on Earth and unable to be reflected back into space. If the amount of greenhouse gases continues to increase, more heat becomes trapped. As a result, Earth's temperature rises, known as global warming, which impacts climate change.

EfekRumahKacaMateri.title: Greenhouse Effect Material
EfekRumahKacaMateri.subtitle: Greenhouse Effect Material
EfekRumahKacaMateri.summary: Step-by-step material summary to understand the greenhouse effect process.
EfekRumahKacaMateri.contentNotAvailable: Material content is not yet available.
EfekRumahKacaMateri.step1Desc: Earth is likened to a greenhouse, while humans, animals, plants, and all living activities within it are likened to plants inside a greenhouse.
EfekRumahKacaMateri.step2Desc: Sunlight enters Earth like rays passing through glass in a greenhouse.
EfekRumahKacaMateri.step3Desc: Some radiation is absorbed by Earth and some is reflected as infrared radiation. When infrared radiation goes upward, it hits the glass; some can escape and some bounces back into the greenhouse.
EfekRumahKacaMateri.step4Desc: This continues to repeat until the temperature inside the greenhouse keeps increasing.
EfekRumahKacaMateri.step5Desc: This condition is similar to what happens on Earth. Heat from the Sun enters the atmosphere. Some radiation is absorbed by Earth and some is reflected as infrared radiation. When infrared radiation goes upward, it hits greenhouse gases. Some infrared radiation can escape, some bounces back into Earth. This reflection and absorption cycle continues to repeat, causing Earth's temperature to rise.
EfekRumahKacaMateri.step6Desc: Greenhouse gases are gases found in Earth's atmosphere that play a role in retaining solar heat so it doesn't all escape back into space. When sunlight reaches Earth's surface, some energy is absorbed and converted into heat, then radiated back as infrared radiation. Greenhouse gases such as water vapor, carbon dioxide (CO₂), methane (CH₄), dinitrogen oxide (N₂O), and CFC absorb and reflect infrared radiation back to Earth's surface, keeping Earth warm and supporting life. However, if greenhouse gas amounts are excessive due to human activities, too much heat becomes trapped, causing global warming.
EfekRumahKacaMateri.step7Desc: Greenhouse gases come from natural processes and human activities, such as water evaporation, respiration of living beings, burning fossil fuels, industrial activities, agriculture, and the use of man-made chemicals. These gases play a role in retaining heat in the atmosphere, but if their amounts are excessive, they can increase Earth's temperature and trigger global warming.

PemanasanGlobal.title: Global Warming & Climate Change
PemanasanGlobal.subtitle: Core Material
PemanasanGlobal.heading: Global Warming & Climate Change
PemanasanGlobal.description: Global warming is the increase in Earth's temperature because solar heat is trapped by greenhouse gases in the atmosphere. As a result of global warming, climate change occurs—changes in weather patterns on Earth. Seasons become unpredictable, extreme rain and heat occur more frequently, polar ice melts, and sea levels rise. Global warming and climate change don't just affect nature, but also human life. Agriculture is disrupted because seasons are hard to predict, and disasters like floods and droughts occur more frequently. Therefore, understanding both concepts is important so we realize that human actions today greatly determine Earth's future conditions.
PemanasanGlobal.weatherDesc: The condition of the air or atmosphere in terms of temperature, sunlight, humidity, or wind speed measured at a specific place for a limited period.
PemanasanGlobal.climateDesc: The condition of the atmosphere, including temperature, humidity, clouds, rain, and sunlight, observed in a region over a longer period (about 30 years).
PemanasanGlobal.impactTitle: Impact of Weather and Climate Change
PemanasanGlobal.tropical1: Changes in the timing of rainy and dry seasons occur.
PemanasanGlobal.tropical2: Droughts occur, plants struggle to grow, clean water becomes scarce, and forest fires become more common.
PemanasanGlobal.tropical3: The air becomes hotter and more humid, affecting human health.
PemanasanGlobal.tropical4: High rainfall causes floods and landslides.
PemanasanGlobal.tropical5: Triggers the proliferation of harmful animals.
PemanasanGlobal.fourSeason1: Increased potential for heat waves, dehydration, heat strokes, and casualties.
PemanasanGlobal.fourSeason2: Shorter winters with rising temperatures, reducing snowy days.
PemanasanGlobal.fourSeason3: Warm and dry autumns, increasing the potential for forest fires.
PemanasanGlobal.fourSeason5: Typhoons, storms, and hail occur more frequently.
PemanasanGlobal.seaLevelDesc: Weather and climate changes across the globe make Earth warmer, causing ice to melt and become seawater. The impacts of rising sea levels include:
PemanasanGlobal.seaLevel2: Mixing of groundwater with seawater, disrupting groundwater supply for drinking, bathing, washing, and other activities.
PemanasanGlobal.seaLevel3: Gradually, coastal cities may become submerged.
PemanasanGlobal.seaLevel4: Small islands may also be submerged.
PemanasanGlobal.seaLevel5: Erosion of cliffs, beaches, and sand dunes in the ocean will occur more frequently.

PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.title: Global Warming Simulation
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.subtitle: Interactive Simulation
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.heading: Global Warming
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.description: Explore from the initial story, map, to each island in one flow.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.intro1: You have just arrived in the year 2065
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.intro2: Look around you, the environment looks different
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.intro3: Let's explore and find out what really happened!
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.introExtra: The world has changed a lot due to the climate crisis. The environment, cities, and human life are no longer as we know them today.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.introChallenge: Challenge: Go to areas A-F to complete the missions!
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.progressSaved: Island progress will be marked as complete.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.islandAStep1: The villagers live in very difficult conditions. Many families lost their homes due to forest fires several years ago.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.islandAStep2: We are trying to rebuild our lives, but the long drought and extremely high temperatures this year make us afraid that a similar disaster will happen again.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.islandBStep1: We salt farmers depend heavily on weather. However, due to global warming and climate change, the weather has become unpredictable. Rain comes suddenly when it should be summer, disrupting the evaporation process and drastically reducing our salt harvest.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.islandBStep2: As salt farmers, we need the Sun's heat to produce salt. But now the weather often changes. Because rain falls unexpectedly, the salt doesn't have time to dry and our harvest is small.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.islandCStep1: The drastic increase in Earth's temperature has caused polar ice to melt rapidly. This ice melting causes sea levels to rise and damages polar ecosystems, including habitats of animals like polar bears and seals.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.islandCStep2: We are worried that the polar future will become even more damaged if Earth's temperature continues to rise in the coming decades.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.islandDStep1: We live near the coast. But now seawater often rises onto land. Due to global warming, polar ice is melting and the sea is getting higher. Our homes are frequently flooded, and life is getting harder.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.islandDStep2: We are worried that if this continues, one day we will no longer be able to live here and will have to leave our homeland.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.islandEStep1: We work as farmers to support our families. But now nature can no longer be predicted. Sometimes rain falls suddenly, then heat comes for a long time. The crops in our fields don't grow well, many are damaged or even die.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.islandEStep2: Due to frequent crop failures, our income has decreased and we struggle to meet daily needs. Not only that, in the future, food scarcity could occur globally.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.islandFStep1: I am a doctor at the local hospital, and last month's heat wave had a devastating effect on our city. Tragically, a number of patients died from heat-related causes.
PemanasanGlobalSimulasi.islandFStep2: I also see an increase in patients with lung diseases and asthma, and I think this is related to pollution and wildfire smoke in the air.

Sertifikat.title: Certificate
Sertifikat.subtitle: Award
Sertifikat.heading: Completion Certificate
Sertifikat.description: Enter a name to display on the certificate, then download as PDF.

ClimateAction.title: Climate Action
ClimateAction.subtitle: Climate Action
ClimateAction.heading: Climate Action
ClimateAction.shortDesc: Encourage students to take real action through directed activities relevant to global warming and climate change issues.
ClimateAction.longDesc: Climate Action is a section that invites students to take real action in facing global warming and climate change. This section contains two main activities. The first activity is designing a Smart Town—designing a smart, environmentally-friendly city that can overcome the impacts of global warming and climate change through the use of technology and sustainability principles. The second activity is a reflection quiz, aimed at helping students review their understanding, evaluate choices made, and foster awareness and commitment as agents of climate change.

SmartTown.title: Smart Town
SmartTown.subtitle: Climate Action
SmartTown.heading: Designing Smart Town
SmartTown.description: This challenge invites students to design a smart, environmentally-friendly city by combining STEM-ESD principles. Focus on realistic and applicable climate change mitigation solutions for the surrounding environment.
SmartTown.confirmWithActions: Are you sure you want to submit these action choices?
SmartTown.confirmWithoutActions: No actions selected yet. Submit anyway?
SmartTown.story1Body: We need your help to face the problems of global warming and climate change.
SmartTown.story2Body1: Global warming and climate change occur due to various human activities that produce greenhouse gases, such as carbon dioxide (CO₂), dinitrogen oxide (N₂O), water vapor (H₂O), and methane (CH₄).
SmartTown.story2Body2: These gases accumulate in the atmosphere and trap the Sun's heat, causing Earth's temperature to rise and triggering climate change.
SmartTown.story2Body3: We need your help to reduce the increasing negative impacts of these problems.
SmartTown.story3Body1: You have 50 coins, use them to build a smart city oriented towards reducing greenhouse gas emissions as effectively as possible.
SmartTown.story4Body1: This mission aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions. The steps you choose will directly affect the rate of global temperature increase.
SmartTown.story4Body2: Therefore, use the right strategies and technologies to create a low-carbon future and prevent worse global warming!
SmartTown.story6Body1: You have successfully completed all missions. Your actions and decisions have a big impact on Earth's future.
SmartTown.story6Body2: Thank you for being part of the Climate Change Agents. Keep being a guardian of the environment and an inspiration to those around you!
SmartTown.action1Detail: In Indonesia, the building sector is also a significant contributor to greenhouse gas emissions. Energy-efficient buildings are designed with good natural ventilation and lighting, using materials that can retain heat, and utilizing more environmentally friendly cooling and lighting technology. All these efforts aim to reduce energy use and suppress emissions to the atmosphere.
SmartTown.action2Detail: Trees and forests play an important role in absorbing carbon dioxide from the air. Additionally, the presence of trees helps maintain clean air quality and provides comfortable green spaces for the community to be active and rest. In Indonesia, tree planting efforts and developing green open spaces are very important to support a healthy and sustainable environment.
SmartTown.action3Detail: Greenhouse gas emissions from the agricultural sector also contribute a significant portion to total emissions in Indonesia. By changing the way farmers plant and manage their land through environmentally friendly farming, minimal soil processing, organic fertilizer use, and reducing fossil fuel-based fertilizers, emissions can be reduced while improving soil fertility and health in the long term.
SmartTown.action4Label: Build clean public transport and electric vehicles
SmartTown.action4Detail: The transportation sector is one of the major contributors to greenhouse gas emissions in Indonesia, especially from gasoline and diesel-powered vehicles. By increasing the use of electric vehicles and expanding and improving environmentally friendly public transportation systems, emissions from conventional vehicles can be reduced and dependence on fossil fuels like oil and gas can be decreased.
SmartTown.action5Detail: In Indonesia, inefficient food processing still causes waste of food materials and energy. By promoting wise ways of processing, storing, and utilizing food to the community, resource use can be reduced, food waste can be decreased, and emissions from food production and distribution processes can be minimized.
SmartTown.action6Label: Replace coal and gas power plants with nuclear
SmartTown.action6Detail: Indonesia has long been heavily dependent on coal and gas-fired power plants. By developing nuclear power plants as a low-carbon energy source, Indonesia has the opportunity to generate large amounts of electricity with much smaller emissions. This step can help reduce dependence on fossil fuels while reducing greenhouse gas emissions.
SmartTown.action7Label: Capture and store carbon with technology
SmartTown.action7Detail: Developing technology to capture and store carbon from fossil fuel power plants and heavy industries can help Indonesia reduce greenhouse gas emissions. With gradual implementation of this technology, emissions released into the atmosphere can be reduced. As carbon emission costs increase, carbon capture technology will become more affordable and widely used.
SmartTown.action8Detail: Renewable energy, such as solar and wind power, is much cleaner than oil, gas, and coal. In Indonesia, which is rich in sunlight and wind potential in several regions, developing this energy is very important to reduce greenhouse gas emissions. As carbon emission costs increase, renewable energy technology will become more affordable and can be utilized more widely.
SmartTown.action9Label: Conduct education in schools about carbon pollution emergencies
SmartTown.action9Detail: Schools have an important role in building young generation awareness about the dangers of carbon pollution and its impact on the environment. By providing early education about global warming, climate change, and ways to reduce carbon footprint, students in Indonesia can grow into change agents who care about Earth and are ready to take real action for a more sustainable future.
SmartTown.action10Label: Implement carbon emission tariffs for the industrial sector
SmartTown.action10Detail: By giving economic value to carbon pollution, industry players and the community will be encouraged to switch to cleaner energy sources. This policy opens opportunities for environmentally friendly technology to be used more widely in Indonesia, so over time the costs become more affordable and emission impacts can be significantly reduced.

Quiz.reviewDesc: Here are the answer details along with the correct choices.

ProfilePengembang.title: Developer Profile
ProfilePengembang.subtitle: Development Team
ProfilePengembang.heading: Computer Simulation Media Developers
ProfilePengembang.description: This team focuses on developing STEM-ESD-based learning media to strengthen environmental literacy and sustainable decision-making through interactive simulations.
ProfilePengembang.collabDesc: Open for research collaboration, learning trials, and simulation-based module development.
